item.title: Building Europe’s geological future - British Geological Survey
item.description: BGS scientists are contributing to a continent-wide initiative that is strengthening collaboration across borders to tackle shared subsurface challenges and unlock new opportunities.

item.title: Delving deep into mountains for future water security - British Geological Survey
item.description: A BGS-led project is using new water-tracing investigation methods, geophysics and modelling to understand how water moves from mountains to the lowlands, helping to improve water security for billions of people.

item.title: Collecting beach pebbles: fun hobby but is it legal? - British Geological Survey
item.description: The UK coastline will be inundated with keen holidaymakers this summer, many of whom will take a memento of their trip home in the shape of a pebble or two — but is it legal?

item.title: Member Webinar | Lessons from the Field: ADM's Experience Advancing Carbon
item.description: On December 15, 2025 the Institute hosted a Member Webinar highlighting key operational lessons learned from ADM’s CCS project in Decatur, Illinois. The session explored the project’s current status and key operational learnings that inform large-scale carbon storage deployment in the United States and globally. The discussion highlighted lessons learned across project design, metallurgy, and […]

item.title: Updated national 3D model will determine the depth to the solid bedrock beneath our feet - British Geological Survey
item.description: The BGS Superficial Deposit Thickness Model now includes an elevation model of geological rockhead for the first time, providing important data for civil engineers, geoscientists and environmental scientists.

item.title: UK’s world-class research organisations launch new collective - British Geological Survey
item.description: The launch of the National Research Organisations Group, brings together more than 100 leaders from government, research, industry and funding organisations.

item.title: Cheshire Geoenergy Observatory releases key geological datasets for UK thermal energy storage scheme design - British Geological Survey
item.description: A new report and data packs from BGS will provide a unique resource for scientists working on the geology and hydrogeology of the Sherwood Sandstone Formation, an important aquifer and reservoir rock.

item.title: Webinar | Live from the UNFCCC Bonn Climate Change Conference
item.description: On 17 June 2026, the Institute hosted a live webinar from the UNFCCC Bonn Climate Change Conference, providing insights into the latest international climate negotiations and their implications for carbon capture and storage (CCS) and broader carbon management efforts on the road to COP31 in Antalya, Türkiye. Reporting directly from Bonn, the Institute’s Senior Global […]

item.title: New geological mapping underway to help manage flooding along the River Tweed - British Geological Survey
item.description: Scientists are surveying the Tweed catchment for the first time in over 100 years to enhance understanding of localised groundwater and improve flood forecasting.

item.title: Geological research in North Sea helping to safeguard subsea cables - British Geological Survey
item.description: New research investigates optimal burial depths for critical subsea cable infrastructre to safeguard from anchor strikes and other hazards

item.title: Progress Report: Recent Updates on Japan's CCS Projects
item.description: Read this insight for an update on Japan's carbon capture and storage (CCS) projects announced in June 2024. Japan has supported the development of CCS through a series of demonstration projects since 2016. Many of these projects were funded by the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry (METI), helping to establish the technical foundations for […]

item.title: Webinar | State of the Art: CCS Technologies 2026 | Tech Compendium Editor’s Insights [Americas]
item.description: [NOTE – For those who are based in APAC, MEA, and/or Europe, this webinar is also being held on July 23 at 5pm Melbourne / 8am London. Link to register for that session] About Interested in exploring the latest trends shaping the carbon capture and storage (CCS) market? Join the Institute for this 30-minute […]

item.title: State of the Art: CCS Technologies 2026
item.description: State of the Art: CCS Technologies 2026

item.title: Member Webinar | Enabling Steel Decarbonisation: The Role of CCUS and Shared Hubs
item.description: About The Institute is delighted to invite you to join our Monthly Member Webinar series, where each month members and friends of the Institute gain insights from fellow members across a broad spectrum of topics in carbon capture and storage. This month’s webinar features BHP, who will share their expertise on steel decarbonisation and the […]

item.title: Carbon Management in an Era of Climate Implementation: Insights from Bonn on the Road to COP31
item.description: Carbon Management in an Era of Climate Implementation: Insights from Bonn on the Road to COP31

item.title: CCS Capacity Building Workshop Poland
item.description: The Global CCS Institute is pleased to host the next Capacity Building Workshop in Poland. This session will bring together selected stakeholders to discuss the current status of CCS in Poland, explore successful business cases from regions where CCS projects are under development, and examine funding mechanisms and key challenges for local emitters. This workshop […]

item.title: Webinar | State of the Art: CCS Technologies 2026 | Tech Compendium Editor’s Insights [APAC, MEA, Europe]
item.description: [NOTE – For those who are based in the Americas this webinar is also being held on Aug 12 at 4pm Washington D.C.. Link to register for that session] About Interested in exploring the latest trends shaping the carbon capture and storage (CCS) market? Join the Institute for this 30-minute webinar as we unpack the […]
